subject: Mathematics
title: Math in Architecture
description: Learn Math in Architecture in one minute.

hook.text: The Sydney Opera House, the Pantheon dome, and the Gherkin skyscraper all rely on mathematical curves.

buildup.text: Domes distribute weight using parabolic and catenary curves—shapes defined by equations.

discovery.text: Modern parametric architecture uses algorithms to generate building shapes from mathematical functions.

twist.text: Gaudi designed the Sagrada Familia using hanging chain models—gravity created the optimal arches.

climax.text: Every arch, vault, and dome you walk under was shaped by mathematics, whether the builder knew it or not.

punchline.text: Buildings stand because their curves obey equations.

quiz.question: What mathematical shape does a hanging chain naturally form?
